D3 Chemistry L5 Evaluate Reference

Photochromism — UV Opens a Ring, Colour Appears

a spiropyran's closed form is colourless; UV breaks the C-O bond, the molecule unfolds into the planar conjugated merocyanine, and the eyepiece goes blue

A spiropyran has a closed sp³ carbon between two heterocycles — no conjugation across, so it's colourless. UV cleaves the C-O bond; the molecule rotates into the planar trans merocyanine form with extended π conjugation — visible absorption around 560 nm, intense purple-blue colour. Heat or visible light closes it again. Same chemistry powers Transitions sunglasses.

Drag UV dose. The forward rate kₚ(UV) opens the ring; thermal reverse k_b closes it. Steady-state concentration of merocyanine + colour swatch tracks the UV level.
